title: Under-Five Mortality Sub-National Inequality Tracking In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for tracking sub-national inequality in under-five mortality rates in low-income countries, linking DHS mortality data, wealth-quintile disaggregation, and intervention-coverage data at district scale to identify where inequality is growing rather than shrinking.
why_it_matters: Under-five mortality halved globally between 1990 and 2022, but sub-national inequality in many countries has widened even as national averages improved. The poorest wealth quintile in many LMICs has under-five mortality rates 2-3x the richest. DHS surveys provide district-level mortality and wealth data, but systematic inequality tracking — identifying where gaps are growing — is not routinely done at sub-national scale.

success_metrics.target: Every inequality estimate links DHS mortality data, wealth-quintile disaggregation, and coverage data with methodology.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against evidence.json and evidence.md.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review for policy claims, and replication for quantitative analyses.
